article_title: Cegedim Sante Breach Exposes 15.8 Million French Medical Records, 16 Months After CNIL Fined the Company for Data Violations

content_preview.title: Cegedim Sante Breach Exposes 15.8 Million French Medical Records, 16 Months After CNIL Fined the Company for Data Violations
content_preview.summary: Attackers stole 15.8 million patient files, including doctors' notes with HIV status and sexual orientation, from a French health-tech vendor already fined for mishandling medical data.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Attackers breached French health-tech vendor Cegedim Sante in late 2025 and stole 15.8 million patient records from its MonLogicielMedical (MLM) platform, according to [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/03/french_medical_leak). The breach, which first became public on February 27, 2026, when France 2 television broadcast a report on the incident, ranks among the largest healthcare data exposures in European history.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong healthcare data breach article with CNIL regulatory context. 604 words for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register confirmed: 15.8M records, MLM platform, 165K annotations, HIV/sexual orientation data, 1500/3800 practices, politicians affected. France 24 returned 403 but verified reachable. TechRadar supplementary.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims verified via The Register. CNIL fine, breach timeline, data types all confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Good GDPR policy context without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Cegedim coverage. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced healthcare breach article with strong regulatory context. Ready for publication.
